params.name: Simbel Fresco
params.description:
On Mar 3, 1233 BC, the sun shone into the Abu Simbel Temple and sprinkled on the statue!
The temple, built during the reign of the Ramesses II on the bank of the Nile, is a symbol of the power of ancient Egypt. The frescos within the temple depict the story of the Pharaoh Ramesses II leading the ancient Egyptians on an expedition to Nubia. Despite being over 3,000 years old, the frescos remain intact and spectacular, offering an impressive glimpse into ancient Egyptian history.
